pages.name: Home
pages.description: Entry point for the open access science journal platform with global search, featured content, and quick entry into discovery and personalization tools.
primary_functions: Run a basic keyword search across all articles via the main search bar.
Access primary sections such as Browse, Journals, Reading Lists, Collections, and Alerts from the header.
View highlighted or featured content such as top-cited articles, recent issues, and key journals.
Provide quick links to help, policies, and contact information in the footer.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit main search form
Click 'Browse' in header or homepage CTA
Click 'Journals' in header or featured journals block
Click a featured article card
Click a featured journal card
Click a recent issue in homepage highlights
Click 'Reading Lists' in header
Click 'Collections' in header
Click 'Alerts' in header
Click 'About' in footer
Click 'Help' in footer
Click 'Policies & Terms' in footer
Click 'Contact' in footer

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Home' link in the global header navigation
Direct entry URL or browser bookmark to the root index.html

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search and discovery page showing article results with rich filters, sorting, alerts, and save actions for reading lists and collections.
primary_functions: Display search results for a given query (e.g., 'neuroscience', 'climate change impact', 'randomized controlled trial cardiovascular').
Filter results by publication year range (e.g., min=2021, max=2022 or 20192024 or 20182023).
Filter results by subject area (e.g., Neuroscience, Environmental Science, Public Health, Medicine, Computer Science, Cardiology).
Filter results by article type (e.g., Research Article, Clinical Trial, Review Article).
Filter results by access type and license (e.g., Open Access, CC BY).
Filter by open science options such as 'Open Data Available'.
Sort results by options such as Citations - High to Low, Publication Date - Newest First, Relevance, and Sample Size - High to Low.
Open an article detail page by clicking on a result title.
Save individual articles directly from the results list to a Reading List (create or choose a list) or to a Collection/Bibliography.
Create and configure a search alert or saved search (e.g., 'ML Health Weekly') from the current query and filter set.

incoming_params.description: Search query text entered by the user, e.g. 'neuroscience'.
Lower bound for the publication year filter.
Upper bound for the publication year filter.
Comma-separated list of subject area enum values to filter by.
Comma-separated list of article type enum values to filter by.
Comma-separated list of access type enum values to filter by.
Comma-separated list of license enum values to filter by.
If true, restrict results to articles that have open data available.
Sort key such as 'relevance', 'citations_desc', 'publication_date_newest', or 'sample_size_desc'.
Page number for paginated results.
Optional ID of a saved search alert; when present, results are loaded via runSavedSearchAlert instead of searchArticles.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an article title in the results list
Click 'Manage alerts' link after creating or editing an alert
Change filters, sort option, or reset pagination

access_methods.description: Accessed via 'Advanced Search' link in the header, which opens this page with default filters.
Reached after submitting a search query from the homepage or other search boxes.
Directly accessible via a URL with queryString and filter parameters, including alertId for saved searches.

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Detailed view of a single article including metadata, metrics, access options, tools for citation and saving, and related datasets.
primary_functions: Display full article metadata: title, authors, journal, volume/issue, publication year, page range, abstract, keywords, subject areas, access type, and license.
Show article-level metrics such as citation count, sample size (for trials), and downloads.
Provide buttons to download or open the article PDF (e.g., 'Download PDF' or 'View PDF').
Provide a link/button to open the HTML full text view of the article.
Offer citation tools, including choosing a citation style (e.g., APA) and copying the formatted citation.
Provide controls to save the article to a Reading List (including creating a new list) and to add the article to a Collection/Bibliography.
Expose a 'Data & Materials' or 'Supplementary Data' tab listing associated datasets, with links to dataset detail pages (including CSV datasets).
Indicate open science badges such as 'Open Data' where applicable.
Link back to the search results via a 'Back to results' control when navigated from search.

incoming_params.description: ID of the article to display.
Optional original queryString used to generate the search results, for 'Back to results' navigation.
Optional encoded filter/sort parameters from search results to reconstruct the previous search.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Full Text HTML' or 'View HTML' link
Click a dataset title in the 'Data & Materials' tab
Click the journal name in article metadata
Click the volume/issue link in article metadata
Click 'Back to results' when arriving from a search results page

access_methods.description: Accessed via article.html?id={articleId} from search results, issues, reading lists, collections, or other internal links.

pages.name: Article Full Text HTML
pages.description: HTML full text reading view of an article, optimized for long-form on-screen reading with inline tools for saving and citing.
primary_functions: Display the complete HTML full text of the selected article with section navigation.
Provide toolbar actions including 'Save to Reading List', 'Add to Collection', 'Cite', 'Download PDF', and 'Back to article details'.
Show article metadata context (title, authors, journal, publication year, page range).
Allow users to scroll and read long review articles (e.g., those spanning 11 or more pages).

incoming_params.description: ID of the article whose full-text HTML should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to article details' in the full-text toolbar

access_methods.description: Accessed via article_fulltext.html?id={articleId} from the article detail page.

pages.name: Journals List
pages.description: Listing and discovery page for all journals on the platform, with search, subject filters, metrics, and sorting to support journal selection decisions.
primary_functions: Display a searchable list of journals with key metadata (title, subject area, impact factor, APC, median review time).
Provide a journal search field to find a specific journal by name (e.g., 'Journal of Environmental Studies').
Filter journals by subject area (e.g., Computer Science, Environmental Science, Medicine).
Sort journals by metrics such as Impact Factor - High to Low, median review time, or APC.
Open an individual journal detail page by clicking on a journal title.
Support navigation from the Browse section (e.g., 'Browse > By Journal').

incoming_params.description: Optional journal search text, e.g. part of a journal title.
Optional comma-separated list of subject area enum values to filter journals.
Sort key, such as 'impact_factor_desc', 'median_review_time_asc', 'apc_asc', or 'title_asc'.
Page number for paginated journal listings.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the title of a journal in the list
Submit journal search form, change filters, or change sorting

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Journals' link in the global header navigation.
Accessed via the 'Browse Journals' link in the footer.
Accessible via journals.html with optional query, subject, sort, and page parameters.

pages.name: Journal Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single journal, providing aims and scope, key metrics, and entry points to issues, articles, and submission information.
primary_functions: Display journal information such as title, aims and scope, editorial board summary, and subject classification.
Show key metrics including impact factor, Article Processing Charge (APC), and median review time.
Provide prominent links/buttons to 'Submission Guidelines' or 'For Authors' (leading to the submission_guidelines.html page).
Provide an 'Issues' tab to browse issues by year and volume/issue (e.g., select year 2023 then 'Volume 12, Issue 4').
Allow searching within the journal2s content and navigating to specific article or issue pages.
Link to open access and licensing information for the journal where relevant.

incoming_params.description: ID of the journal to display.
Optional year to preselect in the Issues tab when loading the page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Submission Guidelines' or 'For Authors' button
Click an issue entry within the 'Issues' tab

access_methods.description: Accessed via journal.html?id={journalId} from the journals list, browse page, homepage highlights, or article metadata.

pages.name: Journal Issue Contents
pages.description: Contents page for a specific journal issue, listing all included articles with types and page ranges, and supporting navigation to article details.
primary_functions: Display issue-level information such as journal name, year, volume, issue number, and publication date.
List all articles in the issue with titles, authors, article type, and page ranges.
Provide filters within the issue, such as filtering by article type (e.g., 'Review Articles').
Allow users to identify long review articles by viewing page ranges (e.g., spans of at least 11 pages).
Open an article detail page when the user clicks an article title.

incoming_params.description: ID of the journal issue whose contents should be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an article title in the issue contents list
Click the journal name or 'Back to journal' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via issue.html?id={issueId} from journal detail, browse, or homepage recent issue links.

pages.name: Submission Guidelines
pages.description: Author instructions and submission requirements for a specific journal, summarizing APCs, timelines, and ethical and formatting requirements.
primary_functions: Display submission guidelines for the selected journal, including article types, formatting, and ethical requirements.
Summarize key submission parameters such as APCs, expected review timelines, and accepted licenses.
Provide links or buttons to start the submission process on an external submission system, if applicable.
Link back to the parent journal detail page for further journal information.

incoming_params.description: ID of the journal whose submission guidelines should be shown.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to journal' link

access_methods.description: Accessed via submission_guidelines.html?journalId={journalId} from the journal detail page.

pages.name: Reading Lists
pages.description: Management interface for user reading lists used for saving and organizing articles to read later.
primary_functions: Display a list of all existing reading lists (e.g., 'Climate Impact Review', 'Env Studies Reviews 2023').
Allow creation of new reading lists and renaming or deleting existing lists.
Show the articles contained in a selected reading list, including titles, journals, years, and basic metadata.
Allow removal of articles from a reading list and navigation to the article detail page.
Act as the destination when users choose 'Reading Lists' from the header or profile menu.

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a reading list to preselect when loading the page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an article title within a reading list
Create a new reading list or rename an existing one, then refresh view

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Reading Lists' link in the header.
Accessed via reading_lists.html with optional readingListId to preselect a list.

pages.name: Collections / Bibliographies
pages.description: Management page for bibliographic collections used for literature reviews, tracking journal diversity, and citation export.
primary_functions: Display all user-defined collections/bibliographies (e.g., 'Urban Air 2018-23').
Allow creation, renaming, and deletion of collections.
Show the list of articles within a selected collection, including source journals to ensure diversity (e.g., at least 3 different journals).
Allow users to remove articles from a collection and open article detail pages.
Provide export tools for collections (e.g., export in citation formats such as RIS, BibTeX, or plain APA list).

incoming_params.description: Optional ID of a collection to preselect when loading the page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click an article in a collection's item list
Create or rename a collection, then refresh the page with that collection selected

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Collections' link in the header.
Accessed via collections.html with optional collectionId to select a specific bibliography.

pages.name: Dataset Detail
pages.description: Landing page for an open dataset associated with an article, providing metadata, file listings, and access to CSV and other files.
primary_functions: Display dataset metadata such as title, description, associated article, authors, license, and format indicators (e.g., CSV).
List associated files and folders, with a control like 'View file list' to expand and show individual files.
Provide download options for the dataset or individual files (including open CSV files).
Show citation or reference instructions for the dataset, if applicable.
Link back to the parent article detail page and indicate the 'Open Data' status.

incoming_params.description: ID of the dataset to display.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Back to article' link on the dataset detail page

access_methods.description: Accessed via dataset.html?id={datasetId} from the 'Data & Materials' tab on an article.

pages.name: Alerts / Saved Searches
pages.description: Management page for saved searches and alerts, allowing users to review, edit, run, or delete alerts such as weekly topic updates.
primary_functions: List all existing alerts and saved searches (e.g., 'ML Health Weekly').
Display each alert2s query, filters, and frequency (e.g., Weekly).
Allow users to edit alert settings such as name and frequency.
Allow deletion of alerts that are no longer needed.
Provide a way to rerun a saved search, linking back into the search_results.html page with the stored parameters.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'View results' or 'Run search' for a specific alert
Click 'Open in Advanced Search' to see and tweak alert filters before running

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Alerts' link in the header.
Accessible directly at alerts.html to manage saved searches.

pages.name: Browse Landing
pages.description: Browsing hub page providing structured navigation options such as by journal, by subject, and by recent issues, with curated lists for exploration.
primary_functions: Offer clear entry points to browse by journal (link to journals.html), by subject area (linking to subject-filtered search_results.html), or by recent issues (linking to recent journal or issue pages).
Surface curated lists such as top-cited subjects, popular journals, and recently updated issues.
Serve as the target for the 'Browse' link in the header and starting point for actions like 'Browse > By Journal'.

incoming_params.description: Optional subject area enum key to highlight when the browse page loads.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Browse by Journal' for a specific subject
Click a subject area 'Top-cited articles' link
Click a recent issue card
Click a popular journal card

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Browse' link in the global header navigation.
Accessible via browse.html with optional subjectArea to pre-focus a discipline.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the platform, its mission, editorial policies, and institutional affiliations.
primary_functions: Explain the platform2s purpose as an academic open access science journal system.
Describe editorial policies at a high level, including the commitment to open access and open science.
Introduce the editorial board, staff, and supporting institutions.
Provide links to detailed policies and contact information.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click link to detailed policies from About content
Click contact link within About content

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'About' link in the footer.
Accessible directly at about.html via bookmarks or external links.

pages.name: Help & FAQ
pages.description: Support and documentation page explaining core site features, workflows, and troubleshooting tips for searches, lists, collections, alerts, and datasets.
primary_functions: Provide step-by-step guidance on using search, filters, and sorting (e.g., finding most-cited or largest-sample articles).
Explain how to use reading lists, collections/bibliographies, and alerts.
Explain how to access PDFs and HTML full text and how to find and use open datasets.
Offer troubleshooting tips and link to the contact page for further support.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Contact support' link within Help content
Click contextual example links demonstrating search usage
Click link from Help section about reading lists
Click link from Help section about collections/bibliographies
Click link from Help section about alerts

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Help' link in the header.
Accessed via the 'Help' link in the footer.

pages.name: Policies & Terms
pages.description: Central location for platform policies, including open access licensing, data sharing, privacy, and terms of use.
primary_functions: Describe open access policies, including supported licenses such as CC BY and how they appear in filters.
Outline data sharing and open science policies, including requirements for open datasets.
Present privacy policy, terms of use, and author and reviewer ethics guidelines.
Link to related resources such as submission guidelines and about page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click link to About page from policies content
Click link from policy text referring to specific journal author guidelines

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Policies & Terms' link in the footer.
Accessible at policies.html for direct reference or bookmarking.

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact and feedback page providing communication channels to the platform's support and editorial teams.
primary_functions: Display contact information (email addresses, mailing address) for general inquiries and editorial queries.
Provide a contact or feedback form for users to submit questions or report issues.
Link to help and policies pages for self-service support options.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Help & FAQ' link from contact page
Click 'Policies & Terms' link from contact page

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Contact' link in the footer.
Accessible at contact.html for direct contact references or external links.

header_links.description: Homepage with global search and featured content
Browse by journal, subject, or recent issues
Open advanced article search and filtering interface
View and manage saved reading lists
Help and FAQs about using the platform

footer_links.description: About the platform and editorial mission
Open access, data, privacy, and usage policies
User help and frequently asked questions
Browse latest articles by publication date
